reasoning: The type confusion is technically real: print_security_risk_notice() at lines 749-757 of class-common.php passes the array returned by get_security_risk_notice() directly to wp_kses(), which expects a string. PHP silently casts the array to the literal string 'Array', so the function produces wrong output. However, this is not a security vulnerability for three reasons: (1) Reachability is false — grep confirms print_security_risk_notice() has zero call sites anywhere in the plugin; it is dead code that is never invoked from any form rendering path. (2) The actual security notice IS correctly displayed via the template hfc-form.php (lines 81-112) and class-settings.php (lines 591-592), both of which directly access $auhfc_security_risk_notice['title'] and ['message'] with proper esc_html() calls. Users who view the taxonomy or article edit forms DO see the warning. (3) There is no attacker-controlled input anywhere in this flow — get_security_risk_notice() returns a hardcoded translated string array with no user data. The claimed reachability ('visible to any user who can view the taxonomy edit form') is incorrect because the broken print_security_risk_notice() wrapper is never called from those forms. This is a dead-code quality defect, not a security issue.
